title: multidevice: key_packages schema stores per-device
description:
Change relay/src/store.rs key_packages table to store one row per (pub_id, device_id) pair instead of one row per pub_id.

Why: currently PRIMARY KEY is pub_id alone, so publishing a KeyPackage from a second device overwrites the first device's package. Multi-device requires N packages per user.

What to do:
- Add device_id TEXT NOT NULL column to key_packages table
- Change PRIMARY KEY from (pub_id) to (pub_id, device_id)
- Write a SQLite migration: ALTER TABLE or CREATE TABLE + INSERT INTO ... SELECT + DROP + RENAME pattern. Existing rows get a synthetic device_id (e.g. 'legacy') to preserve data.
- Update upsert_key_package(pub_id, device_id, data) signature
- Update get_key_package(pub_id, device_id) for point lookups
- Add get_all_key_packages(pub_id) -> Vec<Vec<u8>> for fetching all device packages when adding a group member
- Add delete_key_packages_for_device(pub_id, device_id) for cleanup on disconnect

Acceptance: two devices for the same pub_id can each have a stored KeyPackage simultaneously; migration runs cleanly on a DB with existing rows; all store tests pass.
